notes: Title: Right Addition by Zero on Naturals
Start from repo-local examples before adding retrieval or external corpora.

assumptions: n ranges over Lean's natural numbers `Nat`.
`+` and `0` are the standard addition and zero on `Nat`.
The statement is universally quantified over `n` with no extra hypotheses.
theorem_spec.informal_statement: For every natural number n, adding zero on the right leaves n unchanged.
theorem_spec.paraphrase: Zero is a right identity for addition on the natural numbers.
theorem_spec.title: Right Addition by Zero on Naturals
